name: TRENCH Motion Pack v0
description: Curated E-mu X3 function generator patterns repurposed as TRENCH modulation sources. Sourced from factory Function Generator XML resources.

entries.name: StepSweeper
entries.short_note: 64-step full-range zigzag sweep. Bipolar oscillation from -1.0 to +1.0. Raw stepped motion, no smoothing. Use for aggressive filter sweeps.

entries.name: SharkTooth
entries.short_note: Diminishing sawtooth pattern. 24 steps, alternating sign, amplitude decays to zero. Triggers active on first 24 steps. Good for decaying sweep bursts.

entries.name: Cascading
entries.short_note: Stepped upward cascade with micro-dips. 24 active steps, values climb from 0.0 to 0.15625. All triggers active. Subtle rhythmic ascent.

entries.name: UpToPitch
entries.short_note: Smooth attack ramp to sustained plateau. 5-step rise to 0.75, then hold. Smooth=1 for interpolation. Use for note-on filter open gestures.

entries.name: HorseLaugh
entries.short_note: Rise to +1.0 then rapid bipolar oscillation with decaying positive peaks. Smooth=1. Chaotic vocal-like motion. Best at moderate depth.

entries.name: EightEightRhythm
entries.short_note: 16-step rhythmic Q pattern. Values 0.22-0.84 in first half, near-zero in second. 8 triggers scattered. Sync=0 (free rate). Punchy Q modulation.

entries.name: 16StepShaker
entries.short_note: 16-step bipolar shuffle. Alternating positive/negative with smooth=1. 3 triggers at steps 0, 10, 12. Sync=2 (note sync). Groove Q movement.

entries.name: Syncopatico
entries.short_note: 32-step syncopated pattern. First 16 steps are hard +1/-1 alternation, second 16 are shaped decay. Smooth=1. Aggressive Q rhythm.

entries.name: ClaveAccent
entries.short_note: Clave rhythm via triggers only. 5 triggers in 16-step cycle (3-2 son pattern). Values are flat. Use triggers for Q accent hits, not continuous modulation.

entries.name: ReverseClaveAccent
entries.short_note: Reverse clave (2-3 son). 5 triggers, offset from ClaveAccent. Values flat. Same use: trigger-based Q accents with inverted phrasing.

entries.name: Jittery
entries.short_note: High-rate micro-jitter. 64 steps, values in -0.14 to +0.11 range. Smooth=1, direction=2 (ping-pong). Rate=18.01. Use at low depth for vibrato/wobble.

entries.name: MorseCode
entries.short_note: 24-step dot-dash pattern. Alternating high values and near-zero gaps. No smoothing. Rate=7.0. Use for rhythmic filter gating or stutter effects.

entries.name: OctaveBrown
entries.short_note: 64-step octave-jumping pattern. Values alternate between -0.75, 0.0, +0.375, +0.75. All 64 triggers active. Direction=4 (random). Chaotic pitch-like modulation.
